messages.content: Build a pure Decider for an Invoice aggregate in TypeScript. Export initialState, decide, and evolve. Use a discriminated union for Command, Event, and State, and throw a typed DomainError when issuing an already-issued invoice.
Write a Decider for a Reservation aggregate covering create, confirm, and cancel. Confirming a cancelled reservation must throw. Include the evolve reducer with exhaustive never default.
Explain briefly how a Decider handles a Shipment aggregate, then implement one with dispatch, deliver, and fail transitions in TypeScript with strict types.
Write a GuestStay Decider in TypeScript with checkIn, chargeNight, and checkOut commands. Cannot check out before at least one night is charged.
Build a Subscription Decider covering subscribe, renew, and cancel. Renewing a cancelled subscription must throw. Use pure decide + evolve.
Implement an OrderFulfilment Decider with strict types. Commands: pick, pack, dispatch. Picking an already packed order must throw a typed domain error.
Show me an Oskar-style when reducer plus aggregateStream helper for a LoyaltyAccount in TypeScript. The account tracks point balance and tier.
What's the idiomatic way to fold an event stream into a CashRegister aggregate using an Oskar-style when function? Show a minimal example.
Build an Oskar-style projection with a when reducer for an Invoice view that denormalises issuer and amount. Use aggregateStream.
Write a projection (read model) for a ShipmentTracker view in TypeScript. Use project(view, event) switching on event.type; events outside the view's interest return the view unchanged.
Show a read-model projection for a SubscriptionStatus view. Ignore events the view does not care about. TypeScript with discriminated unions.
Implement a projection that builds an InvoiceLedger view summing outstanding balances per customer. TypeScript, strict types, exhaustive switch.
Implement a snapshot-with-versioned-upgrade loader for a GuestStay aggregate. CURRENT_VERSION is 3, with v1-to-v2 and v2-to-v3 upgraders. TypeScript, strict types.
Add versioned snapshot upgrading for a Subscription aggregate. CURRENT_VERSION is 4 with three upgraders. Use a per-version map.
How do I snapshot a Reservation aggregate with a versioned upgrade pipeline in TypeScript? Show a minimal working example.
Write an append-with-optimistic-concurrency function for an Invoice stream. Use a local EventStore interface and throw a typed ConcurrencyConflict on mismatch.
Refactor an unsafe blind-append to use optimistic concurrency via expectedRevision. The aggregate is a CashRegister. Use a local EventStore interface.
Build a generic concurrency-safe appender in TypeScript. It must accept any stream, read its latest revision, and append with expectedRevision. Typed ConcurrencyConflict error class.
Build a CommandHandler for a Reservation aggregate that loads events, calls decide, and writes back via appendToStream with expectedRevision. Local EventStore interface, no external imports.
Write a CommandHandler over an EventStore for an Invoice aggregate. The handler should load, decide, and append. Wrap the IO boundary in Effect.gen.
Implement a generic CommandHandler factory that takes a Decider and returns an async (streamName, command) => Promise<void>. Use a local EventStore interface.
Convert this imperative handler into a CommandHandler that orchestrates over a local EventStore interface. The aggregate is a Shipment.
Build a subscription runner that resumes a projection from a persisted checkpoint. Include a CheckpointStore interface and crash-safe ordering (apply then write).
Explain briefly how a checkpointed subscription works then implement one in TypeScript using Effect.gen for the IO boundary.
Add resumable checkpointing to this polling subscription. The store must expose read and write, and writes happen after successful projection apply.
Show the idiomatic generic aggregateStream<S, E> helper in TypeScript that folds an AsyncIterable of events into state. Must not mutate the input.
Write Oskar-style when + aggregateStream for a CashRegister with typed check guard in TypeScript. Use data property under each event.
Build Oskar-style when + aggregateStream for a GuestStay aggregate tracking nights and status. TypeScript, strict, data-property convention.
Add an upcast function to migrate old SubscriptionSubscribed v1 events (with planName) into current shape (with plan and tier). TypeScript, unknown input, default throws.
Show me how to upcast old Invoice events so the aggregate only ever sees the current shape. Old InvoiceIssued v1 has issuedBy as string; current v2 has issuer: { id, name }.
Implement a read-model rebuild routine that wipes the projection store and re-projects from revision 0 in batches. Report progress. Resumable via a from option. TypeScript, local interfaces.
Write a chunked read-model rebuild for the LoyaltyAccount view. It should resume from a cursor and apply a project function per event.
Build a minimal CQRS boundary: handleCommand uses a Decider and EventStore; queryView reads from a separate read store. The two sides share the event type but not the state type.
Write a CQRS example for an OrderFulfilment aggregate. The write side uses a Decider and EventStore; the read side exposes a denormalised OrderTracker view. Eventually consistent.
Write a stateless process manager that reacts to OrderConfirmed by emitting ReserveStock, and to StockReserved by emitting ChargePayment. TypeScript, pure react function.
Build a stateful process manager for a Shipment-to-Invoice saga. Encode it as (state, event) => { nextState, command? }.
Implement an integration-event translation for a Reservation domain. Map ReservationConfirmed to a versioned IntegrationEvent; internal events must translate to null.
Build an outbox-append helper that atomically writes domain events and integration outbox rows in one transaction. A worker elsewhere publishes the rows.
Build a command handler for a Reservation that uses Effect.gen to orchestrate EventStoreDB-style reads and appendToStream writes with expectedRevision.
Show a subscription runner wrapped in Effect.gen that reads from a checkpoint, processes events in a handler, and writes the checkpoint after each event.
